title: syzygium calcicola
intro: Syzygium calcicola, also known as the paperbark maple, is a species of flowering plant in the myrtle family, native to the tropical regions of northern Australia and southern New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and has been used to treat various health issues such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The health benefits of Syzygium calcicola include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
intro_study: According to "Plants (Basel, Switzerland)", syzygium calcicola, like other Syzygium plants, is rich in antioxidant phenolic compounds which can act as multi-target neuroprotective agents against Alzheimer's disease.
